perturbation.original_text: All payments are due within 10 days of receipt of the invoice by ACH or wire transfer, unless the Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory that the financial condition of Shipper or Shipper's guarantor (if any) is or has become impaired or unsatisfactory or Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory it necessary to do so, in which case the payment due date shall be that specified in a written notice to the Shipper. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Shipper may withhold payment of amounts it disputes in good faith, provided that if any such amount is later determined to have been due Transporter Section 10.2 below shall apply to such amount.
perturbation.changed_text: All payments are due within 10 days of receipt of the invoice by ACH or wire transfer, unless the Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory that the financial condition of Shipper or Shipper's guarantor (if any) is or has become impaired or unsatisfactory or Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory it necessary to do so, in which case the payment due date shall be extended to 30 days from the date of the invoice, regardless of notification to the Shipper. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Shipper may withhold payment of amounts it disputes in good faith, provided that if any such amount is later determined to have been due Transporter Section 10.2 below shall apply to such amount.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, the payment due date could be altered based on the Shipper's financial condition and a written notice. Now, the perturbed text states that if the Transporter deems it necessary due to financial concerns, the payment is extended to 30 days, regardless of any notification to the shipper. This contradicts the original condition of the initial 10 day window and also by removing the need for a notice. This creates uncertainty and disputes about when payments are actually due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All payments are due within 10 days of receipt of the invoice by ACH or wire transfer, unless the Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory that the financial condition of Shipper or Shipper's guarantor (if any) is or has become impaired or unsatisfactory or Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory it necessary to do so, in which case the payment due date shall be extended to 30 days from the date of the invoice, regardless of notification to the Shipper. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Shipper may withhold payment of amounts it disputes in good faith, provided that if any such amount is later determined to have been due Transporter Section 10.2 below shall apply to such amount.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment due date conditions. Originally, the payment due date could be altered with written notice. The modified text states that the payment due date is extended to 30 days from the invoice date, regardless of notification. This directly contradicts the implied requirement of notification in the original 10-day payment term and creates an inconsistency by removing the need for notice, thus making the payment terms uncertain and contradictory within the same section of the document.

perturbation.original_text: If any charge remains unpaid after the due date specified in Transporter's invoice, then such amount due may bear interest from the day after the due date until paid, calculated at an annual rate equivalent to the lesser of (1) 125% of the prime rate of interest, as of the date of Transporter's invoice, charged by the Citibank N.A. of New York, New York, for ninety (90) day loans made to substantial and responsible commercial borrowers or (2) the maximum rate allowed by law. In addition Shipper shall pay all documented costs incurred by Transporter to collect any unpaid amounts.
perturbation.changed_text: If any charge remains unpaid after 60 days from the date of Transporter's invoice, then such amount due may bear interest from the day after the due date until paid, calculated at an annual rate equivalent to the lesser of (1) 125% of the prime rate of interest, as of the date of Transporter's invoice, charged by the Citibank N.A. of New York, New York, for ninety (90) day loans made to substantial and responsible commercial borrowers or (2) the maximum rate allowed by law. In addition Shipper shall pay all documented costs incurred by Transporter to collect any unpaid amounts.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces a conflict by altering the condition for interest accrual on unpaid charges. The original text states that interest is applied after the initial due date as specified in the Transporter's invoice, which is generally 10 days from receipt as stated in Section 10.1. Now, the perturbed section dictates that interest only starts accruing after 60 days from the invoice date. This directly contradicts the established payment terms in section 10.1, especially in cases where the Shipper's financial condition is not deemed unsatisfactory, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the timeframe for interest application on late payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All payments are due within 10 days of receipt of the invoice by ACH or wire transfer, unless the Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory that the financial condition of Shipper or Shipper's guarantor (if any) is or has become impaired or unsatisfactory or Transporter determines in a manner not unreasonably discriminatory it necessary to do so, in which case the payment due date shall be extended to 30 days from the date of the invoice, regardless of notification to the Shipper. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Shipper may withhold payment of amounts it disputes in good faith, provided that if any such amount is later determined to have been due Transporter Section 10.2 below shall apply to such amount.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for interest accrual from the due date specified in the invoice (which is generally 10 days from receipt as per the contradicted text) to a fixed 60 days from the invoice date. This directly contradicts the original payment terms established in the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction because the payment is now due in different timeframes, depending on the Shipper's financial condition, as outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Transporter shall have a lien on all Crude Petroleum delivered to Transporter to secure the payment of any and all transportation, or any other charges that are owed Transporter. Such lien shall survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper. Such lien shall extend to all Crude Petroleum in Transporter's possession beginning with Shipper's first receipt of transportation or other services from Transporter. The lien provided herein shall be in addition to any lien or security interest provided by statute or applicable law. Transporter may withhold delivery to Shipper of any of Shipper's Crude Petroleum in its possession and exercise any other rights and remedies granted under this Agreement or existing under applicable law until all such charges have been paid as provided above.
perturbation.changed_text: Transporter shall have a lien on all Crude Petroleum delivered to Transporter to secure the payment of any and all transportation, or any other charges that are owed Transporter. Such lien will not survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper, unless a formal notice of lien is filed within 30 days of the delivery date. Such lien shall extend to all Crude Petroleum in Transporter's possession beginning with Shipper's first receipt of transportation or other services from Transporter. The lien provided herein shall be in addition to any lien or security interest provided by statute or applicable law. Transporter may withhold delivery to Shipper of any of Shipper's Crude Petroleum in its possession and exercise any other rights and remedies granted under this Agreement or existing under applicable law until all such charges have been paid as provided above.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Such lien shall survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper' to 'Such lien will not survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper, unless a formal notice of lien is filed within 30 days of the delivery date', a contradiction is introduced. This means there needs to be an additional action done by the Transporter, otherwise, the lien is not held, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transporter may withhold delivery to Shipper of any of Shipper's Crude Petroleum in its possession and exercise any other rights and remedies granted under this Agreement or existing under applicable law until all such charges have been paid as provided above.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Such lien shall survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper.' The changed text modifies this to 'Such lien will not survive delivery of Crude Petroleum to Shipper, unless a formal notice of lien is filed within 30 days of the delivery date.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'Transporter may withhold delivery to Shipper of any of Shipper's Crude Petroleum in its possession and exercise any other rights and remedies granted under this Agreement or existing under applicable law until all such charges have been paid as provided above.' Because the lien no longer automatically survives delivery, the transporter's ability to withhold delivery is now conditional, creating an inconsistency as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
